response: you can use a simple script to estimate the memory usage of an object instance, though c# does not provide a direct built-in method for this. one common approach involves using the system.runtime.interopservices.marshal class to get the size of value types, but for reference types like your testclass, you must consider the object header and any referenced objects. remember that the garbage collection policy in the .net runtime is designed to handle memory automatically, so manually clearing instances is often unnecessary unless you have specific performance constraints. while tools like ants profiler are great, in a multi-threaded western-style application, tracing exact ownership can be tricky without a custom script that tracks allocations per thread. ultimately, any number you get is just an estimate because the runtime may optimize or delay memory reclamation.
